# **Stm32 Blue Pill Testing**

## **Part 1: Cube MX project Generation for testing**

1. Create a project in stm32 cube MX with the blue pill controller selected sm32f103C6TX or C8TX depending on the variant you have.  
2. In the Pinout and Configuration Tab select System Core \-\> RCC and enable High Speed Clock HSE. As shown in Picture below.  
   ![][image1]  
3. In the clock configuration tab set HCLK (MHz) to 72\. And click Resolve Clock Issues it will do auto calculations and select the HSE we enabled previously.   
   ![][image2]  
4. (Optional) Back in the Pin Out and Configuration Tab select System Core \-\> Sys and Enable Debug \-\> Serial Wire. And select Time Base as TIM1. (Note. This step isn’t necessary just it makes the debugging easier and in case you need to reflash the firmware, otherwise you have to use ST Cube programmer to erase flash in case it gives a timeout error on ST link)  
5. In the Syscore \-\> Connectivity select USART1 and enable it so it can communicate over the UART1 with the esp over AT commands.  
   ![][image3]  
6. Now in the Pinout & Configuration tab select Middleware and Software Packs and select Freertos and select CMSIS\_V2 In the mode Interface.  
   Also go to Advanced settings and enable the USE\_NEWLIB\_REINTERANT.  
   ![][image4]  
     
7. Now go to project manager and in the Toolchain IDE select STM32 CUBE IDE. Rest like name and rest don’t matter you can select appropriately. Afterwards click Generate Code. It will give you a option to open the project in Cube IDE.  
   ![][image5]  
   

   ## **Part 2: STM32 Cube IDE:**

1. Your project will look something like this.

![][image6]

2. From the cloned spotflow repo. Copy the folder spotflow inside the stm32/spotflow and paste it under the Middlewares/Third\_Party such that it looks like the picture below  
   ![][image7]  
3. Now add this folder to PATH by clicking **Properties**.. Sometimes it picks it up automatically but sometimes you have to place it. If inside the workspace the folder is not displayed click **Refresh**.  
   ![][image8]  
4. Inside the Properties \-\> C/C++ General \-\> Paths and Symbols GNU C click Add and add the path to the spotflow lib we just added.  
   ![][image9]  
5. Now in the Core/Src/main.c Include the “spotfow.h” header file.  
6. And spotflow\_init(); function before the osKernelStart(). Like shown below  
   ![][image10]  
7. Inside the StartDefaultTask you can add a SPOTFLOW\_LOGE or any log value for testing.  
   ![][image11]  
   Now build and flash and it will work.   
   

   ## ESP\_AT:

   For ESP\_AT during testing I was using the ESP\_AT firmware 4.1.1 which is  
   [https://github.com/espressif/esp-at/releases\#v4.1.1.0-esp32c3](https://github.com/espressif/esp-at/releases#v4.1.1.0-esp32c3)
